code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples into a single fluent, compact sentence.
    Information belonging to the same subject is merged into one clause.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Mapping from predicate to a readable phrase template.
    phrase_map = {
        "country": "is based in {obj}",
        "foundingDate": "was founded on {obj}",
        "industry": "operates in the {obj} industry",
        "city": "is located in {obj}",
        "netIncome": "has a net income of {obj}",
        "operatingIncome": "has an operating income of {obj}",
        "regionServed": "serves the region {obj}",
        "areaTotal": "covers a total area of {obj}",
        "leader": "is led by {obj}",
        "leaderTitle": "who holds the title {obj}",
        "populationDensity": "has a population density of {obj}",
        "foundationPlace": "was founded at {obj}",
        "numberOfEmployees": "employs {obj} people",
        "numberOfLocations": "has {obj} locations",
        "service": "provides {obj}",
        "type": "is a {obj}",
        "alternativeName": "also known as {obj}",
        "birthDate": "was born on {obj}",
        "keyPerson": "has key person {obj}",
        "cost": "costs {obj}",
        "location": "is located in {obj}",
        "product": "produces {obj}",
        "subsidiary": "has subsidiary {obj}",
        "timeZone": "lies in the {obj}",
        "isPartOf": "is part of {obj}",
        "parentCompany": "is owned by {obj}",
        "capital": "has capital {obj}",
        "motto": "has the motto {obj}",
        "officialLanguage": "official language is {obj}",
        "leaderParty": "belongs to the party {obj}",
        "areaUrban": "has an urban area of {obj}",
        "part": "includes part {obj}",
        "revenue": "earned revenue of {obj}",
        "ethnicGroup": "has ethnic group {obj}",
        "governmentType": "has a government type of {obj}",
        "percentageOfAreaWater": "has water area percentage of {obj}",
        "longName": "full name is {obj}",
        "populationTotal": "population totals {obj}",
        "language": "language is {obj}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "elevation is {obj} metres above sea level",
    }

    # Helper to pretty‑print numbers (adds commas).
    def fmt_number(val: str) -> str:
        stripped = val.replace(",", "")
        if stripped.isdigit():
            return f"{int(stripped):,}"
        return val

    # Group triples by their subject.
    from collections import defaultdict
    groups = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        groups[t.subject].append(t)

    subject_clauses = []

    for subject, items in groups.items():
        # Detect leader + leaderTitle combination.
        leader_obj = None
        title_obj = None
        other_phrases = []

        for t in items:
            if t.predicate == "leader":
                leader_obj = fmt_number(t.object)
            elif t.predicate == "leaderTitle":
                title_obj = fmt_number(t.object)
            else:
                tmpl = phrase_map.get(t.predicate, "{obj}")
                phrase = tmpl.format(obj=fmt_number(t.object))
                other_phrases.append(phrase)

        # Build the clause for this subject.
        parts = []

        if leader_obj:
            lead_phrase = f"is led by {leader_obj}"
            if title_obj:
                lead_phrase += f", who holds the title {title_obj}"
            parts.append(lead_phrase)
        elif title_obj:
            parts.append(f"who holds the title {title_obj}")

        parts.extend(other_phrases)

        # Join parts with commas and an "and" before the last part.
        if not parts:
            clause = subject
        elif len(parts) == 1:
            clause = f"{subject} {parts[0]}"
        else:
            clause = f"{subject} " + ", ".join(parts[:-1]) + ", and " + parts[-1]

        subject_clauses.append(clause)

    # Combine subject clauses into final sentence.
    if len(subject_clauses) == 1:
        sentence = subject_clauses[0] + "."
    else:
        sentence = ", ".join(subject_clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + subject_clauses[-1] + "."

    return sentence

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
